text: State-of-the-art machine learning regressors were trained using 80,038 experimental RTs from the METLIN small molecule dataset.
source_excerpt:
We have trained state-of-the-art machine learning regressors using the 80,038 
experimental RTs from the METLIN small molecule dataset (SMRT)

super_pattern.subject: machine learning regressors
super_pattern.relation: trained on experimental retention times from METLIN dataset

text: 5,666 molecular descriptors were generated with the alvaDesc software.
source_excerpt:
5,666 molecular descriptors and 2,214 fingerprints (MACCS166, Extended Connectivity, and Path Fingerprints fingerprints) were generated with
 the alvaDesc software

text: 2,214 fingerprints were generated with the alvaDesc software.
source_excerpt:
5,666 molecular descriptors and 2,214 fingerprints (MACCS166, Extended Connectivity, and Path Fingerprints fingerprints) were generated with
 the alvaDesc software

text: A heavily regularized DNN trained with cosine annealing warm restarts and stochastic weight averaging achieved mean absolute error of 39.2±1.2 s.
source_excerpt:
The best results were obtained by a heavily regularized DNN trained with 
cosine annealing warm restarts and stochastic weight averaging, achieving a 
mean and median absolute errors of 39.2±1.2 s and 17.2 ± 0.9 s, respectively

super_pattern.subject: heavily regularized DNN with cosine annealing and stochastic weight averaging

text: A novel Bayesian meta-learning approach is proposed for RT projection between CMs.
source_excerpt:
A novel Bayesian meta-learning approach is proposed for RT projection between
 CMs from as few as 10 molecules while still obtaining competitive error rates 
 compared with previous approaches

text: The Bayesian meta-learning approach requires as few as 10 molecules for RT projection between chromatographic methods.
source_excerpt:
A novel Bayesian meta-learning approach is proposed for RT projection between
 CMs from as few as 10 molecules while still obtaining competitive error rates 
 compared with previous approaches

super_pattern.context: retention time projection between chromatographic methods
